title: My Family
sentences: This is my family.
I have a mother, a father, and a little brother.
My brother is four years old.
We love to play together in the park.

questions.q: How old is the brother?
Why do you think families like to play together in the park?
choices: It is fun and they are together
It is the only game they know
They do not like their house

title: A Big Trip
sentences: We are going on a big trip this summer.
First we fly to Singapore.
Then we visit Malaysia and Indonesia.
I am very excited to see new animals and new places.

questions.q: How many countries do they visit?
Why do you think the child feels excited before the trip?
choices: Because everything is new and different
Because there is nothing to see

title: The Merlion
sentences: The Merlion is a famous statue in Singapore.
It has the head of a lion and the body of a fish.
Water comes out of its mouth.
Many tourists take photos next to it.

questions.q: What is the Merlion's body?
Why do you think so many tourists want a photo with the Merlion?
choices: It is a famous and unusual statue
It is far from the city

title: The Orangutan
sentences: Orangutans live in the forests of Borneo.
They have long arms and orange fur.
They eat fruit, leaves, and insects.
Baby orangutans stay with their mothers for many years.

questions.q: What color is an orangutan's fur?
Why do you think baby orangutans stay with their mothers for so long?
choices: To learn how to survive in the forest
Because they do not like fruit

title: Hawker Food
sentences: In Singapore, people eat at hawker centers.
A hawker center has many food stalls in one place.
You can eat chicken rice, noodles, or satay.
Everyone sits together at big tables.

questions.q: What is chicken rice?
Why do you think hawker centers put many food stalls in one place?
choices: So everyone can choose something different and eat together
Because there is only one kind of food

title: The River Safari
sentences: We took a boat on the Kinabatangan River.
We saw a proboscis monkey with a big nose.
We also saw a crocodile near the water.
It was a quiet and exciting adventure.

questions.q: What animal has a big nose?
Why do you think a boat is a good way to see animals on the river?
choices: It is quiet and does not scare the animals away

title: The Petronas Towers
sentences: The Petronas Towers are in Kuala Lumpur.
They are two tall towers, side by side.
For a few years, they were the tallest towers in the world.
At night, the towers shine with bright lights.

questions.q: How many towers are there?
Why do you think the towers shine with lights at night?
choices: To look beautiful and be seen from far away
Because lights are forbidden during the day

title: The Rice Fields
sentences: In Ubud, there are green rice fields on the hills.
Farmers built them like steps, one above the other.
Water flows from the top field to the bottom field.
The fields look like a giant green staircase.

questions.q: Where are the rice fields built?
Why do you think farmers build the rice fields like steps?
choices: So water can flow down from field to field
So the water cannot move at all
Because steps look nicer than flat fields
Because rice does not need water

title: Monkey Forest
sentences: Monkey Forest is a park in Ubud full of monkeys.
The monkeys are not afraid of people.
Sometimes a monkey takes food from your hand.
Visitors must hold their hats and sunglasses tight.

questions.q: What might a monkey take from you?
Why do you think visitors must hold their hats and sunglasses tight?
choices: Because the monkeys are used to people and may grab things
Because there is a lot of wind
Because hats are not allowed in the park

title: The Green Turtle
sentences: Green turtles swim in the sea near Gili Air.
They can hold their breath for a long time.
Green turtles eat seagrass and algae.
They always return to the beach where they were born.

questions.q: What do green turtles eat?
Why do you think green turtles always return to the beach where they were born?
choices: It is a safe and familiar place to lay their eggs
The sea pushes them back every time

title: The Island Without Cars
sentences: Gili Air is a small island with no cars.
People walk, ride bikes, or use a cidomo.
A cidomo is a small cart pulled by a horse.
The island is quiet and very peaceful.

questions.q: What pulls a cidomo?
Why do you think a car-free island is good for children?
choices: It is quieter and safer to walk and play
There is nothing to do without cars
Islands need cars to be interesting

title: A Day at the Beach
sentences: Today we are going to the beach in Sanur.
The sand is soft and the water is warm.
We swim, build sandcastles, and eat fresh fruit.
In the evening, we watch the sun go down.

questions.q: What do they build at the beach?
Why do you think watching the sun go down is a nice way to end the day?
choices: It is calm and colorful, a peaceful moment
It is the noisiest part of the day

title: The Water Park
sentences: Waterbom is a big water park in Bali.
It has fast slides and a lazy river.
Children and grown-ups both have fun there.
After the slides, everyone is happy and wet.

questions.q: What does the water park have?
Why do you think both children and grown-ups enjoy the water park?
choices: Playing with water and going down slides is fun at any age
The park is only open at night

title: Packing a Suitcase
sentences: Before a trip, we pack our suitcase.
We put in clothes, a toothbrush, and a hat.
We also pack sunscreen for hot, sunny days.
A camera is important too, for all the memories.

questions.q: Why do they pack sunscreen?
Why do you think it is a good idea to pack a camera on a trip?
choices: To keep memories of the places you visit
Cameras are needed to pack a suitcase

title: A Long Flight
sentences: The flight from Barcelona to Singapore is very long.
It takes more than thirteen hours.
We watch movies, eat meals, and try to sleep.
When we land, it feels wonderful to walk again.

questions.q: How long is the flight?
Why do you think it feels wonderful to walk again after landing?
choices: You have been sitting still for many hours
Walking is not allowed on the plane

title: The Sun Bear
sentences: The sun bear is the smallest bear in the world.
It lives in the forests of Borneo.
The sun bear has a very long tongue.
It uses its tongue to eat honey from tree trunks.

questions.q: What does the sun bear eat with its long tongue?
Why does the sun bear need a long tongue?
choices: To reach honey deep inside tree trunks

title: Batu Caves
sentences: Batu Caves is near Kuala Lumpur.
You climb many colorful stairs to reach the cave.
Inside the cave, there is a beautiful temple.
Monkeys live all around the stairs and the cave.

questions.q: What do you climb to reach the cave?
Why do you think visitors should be careful with their belongings near the monkeys?
choices: The monkeys are used to people and may take things
Monkeys never come near the stairs
Monkeys live inside the temple only
There are no monkeys at Batu Caves

title: Market Day
sentences: The market in Ubud is full of colors and sounds.
Sellers offer fruit, spices, and handmade crafts.
You can smell sweet mangoes and fresh coconuts.
It is a fun place to walk and look around.

questions.q: What can you smell at the market?
Why do you think markets are full of colors and sounds?
choices: Many sellers and fresh products are all together in one place
Sounds and colors are forbidden elsewhere

title: Time Zones
sentences: When we travel east, the clock changes.
Singapore is many hours ahead of Barcelona.
Bali is one hour ahead of Kuala Lumpur.
It takes a few days for our bodies to feel normal again.

questions.q: Is Singapore ahead or behind Barcelona?
Why do you think our bodies need a few days to feel normal after changing time zones?
choices: The body's clock needs time to adjust to new hours
It happens instantly with no effort
Time zones do not affect sleep

title: Coming Home
sentences: After many weeks, our trip is coming to an end.
We pack our suitcase full of memories and photos.
We say goodbye to new friends and new places.
Soon we will tell everyone at home about our adventure.

questions.q: What do they pack besides clothes?
Why do you think it feels good to tell others about a trip when you get home?
choices: Sharing the adventure lets you relive it and connect with others
Nobody wants to hear about trips
Talking about a trip erases the memories
